text: As a subject that nourishes intellectually and morally, the Islamic Religious Education (PAI) is where students are educated on beliefs and morality in Islam. The subject matter of PAI that was agreed on by many Muslim scholars relates to belief and morality in Islam. Belief (akidah) pertains to believing in The One and Only Allah SWT. Morality (akhlak) pertients to ways in which people behave before Allah SWT and to fellow humans. Belief and morality are understood as basic Islamic knowledge required for students to become human resources who have mastered various knowledge, intelligence, faith, civility, kindness, humanity, creativity, inventiveness, innovativeness, become highly efficient, being competitive, are productive and are capable of advancing the country, therefore it is imperative to expose the threat of globalization and technology through social media in an attempt to keep the Islamic values embedded in education. Histories of various Islamic figures such as the Prophets are proven to have lived in line with beliefs and morality according to the Quran and Hadith, which calls for Muslims to embody morality while forbidding improprieties. Hence, the existence of belief and morality in PAI certainly has the intention to refine the student’s character. This study intends to reveal how moral education that forms the character of students by exploring and understanding the concept of belief and morality and the method of their presentation in Islamic Religious Education, so as to generate a generation that is accountable, which possesses values and that is highly competitive, as well as to strengthen the concept of belief and morality in Islamic Religious Education for the purposes of shaping a generation that is dignified, intelligent, advanced and of quality, therefore more effective educational policies and students with morality will be produced.
